Bash(1 <<'PYTHON_CODE'
from apps.regime.infrastructure.models import RegimeLogModel

print\('=== Regime Data from PostgreSQL ===\\n'\)

# Total count
total = RegimeLogModel.objects.count\(\)
print\(f'Total regime records: {total}'\)
print\(\)

# Get recent records
recent = RegimeLogModel.objects.order_by\('-observed_at'\)[:30]
print\(f'Recent 30 records:\\n'\)

for r in recent:
    print\(f'ID:{r.id:4d} | Observed:{r.observed_at} | Regime:{r.dominant_regime:12s} | Conf:{r.confidence:.3f} | Z:\({r.growth_momentum_z:+.2f}, {r.inflation_momentum_z:+.2f}\)'\)
    if r.distribution:
        print\(f'       Distribution: {r.distribution}'\)

# Group by dominant regime
print\('\\n=== Regime Distribution ===\\n'\)
from django.db.models import Count
regime_counts = RegimeLogModel.objects.values\('dominant_regime'\).annotate\(count=Count\('id'\)\).order_by\('-count'\)
for item in regime_counts:
    print\(f"{item['dominant_regime']:12s}: {item['count']}"\)

# Check for any anomalies
print\('\\n=== Potential Issues ===\\n'\)

# Check for records with very low confidence
low_conf = RegimeLogModel.objects.filter\(confidence__lt=0.3\).count\(\)
print\(f'Records with low confidence \(<0.3\): {low_conf}'\)

# Check for records with very high confidence
high_conf = RegimeLogModel.objects.filter\(confidence__gt=0.95\).count\(\)
print\(f'Records with very high confidence \(>0.95\): {high_conf}'\)

# Check date range
from django.db.models import Min, Max
dates = RegimeLogModel.objects.aggregate\(Min\('observed_at'\), Max\('observed_at'\)\)
print\(f'Date range: {dates}'\)
PYTHON_CODE)
